id: Rongotai Labour Film Evening-29 Wigan Street, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Humanitix
name: Rongotai Labour Film Evening
venue: 29 Wigan Street, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
long_description:
For everyone who missed the showing of "Prime Minister' - a film about the extraordinary term of Jacinda Adern's leadership of New Zealand - at the NZIFF, Rongotai Labour is pleased to secure a showing to help to raise funds for the 2026 election campaign.
This film chronicles Jacinda Adern's tenure as Prime Minister and provides an insight into both the political and personal challenges Jacinda faced during those six years. The inclusion of considerable footage from Jacinda's partner Clarke Gayford provides a deeply personal view of life as the Prime Minister in very challenging times.
(Los Angeles Times review) 'Prime Minister' is not interested in wrangling, dealing and lawmaking, or even the nuts and bolts or her progressive views. .. But in the fleet, pacey manner of the editing, toggling between private and public moments with highlight-reel efficiency, the film is a stirring glimpse of top-down kindness as a winning leadership style.

But in the fleet, pacey manner of the editing, toggling between private and public moments with highlight-reel efficiency, the film is a stirring glimpse of top-down kindness as a winning leadership style.

id: Downton Abbey- The Grand Finale movie fundraiser-52 Beach Street, Petone, Lower Hutt 5012, New Zealand-Humanitix
name: Downton Abbey- The Grand Finale movie fundraiser
venue: 52 Beach Street, Petone, Lower Hutt 5012, New Zealand
long_description: Come and watch the movie we have all been waiting for, and support Faultline Chorus at the same time! We would love to see you- join us for a drink, a chat, a raffle and a great movie!

id: Capital City Big Band-2 Hill Street, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Humanitix
name: Capital City Big Band
venue: 2 Hill Street, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
long_description: Capital City Big Band (or CCBB for short) is a high level ensemble consisting of a mixture of professional and top community musicians in Wellington. They will be performing classic big band works in the art deco Wellington Cathedral of St Paul
description:
Capital City Big Band (or CCBB for short) is a high level ensemble consisting of a mixture of professional and top community musicians in Wellington.
They will be performing classic big band works in the art deco Wellington Cathedral of St Paul

id: TEQ Talks | Wellington Edition-Kelburn Parade, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Humanitix
name: TEQ Talks | Wellington Edition
venue: Kelburn Parade, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
long_description:
VES NZ & the VFX Guild present
 TEQ Talks | Wellington Edition
Wednesday 18 September
6:00 – 9:00pm
Drinks at 6pm & Talks from 7pm
Victoria University of Wellington – Te Aro Campus
Networking drinks to follow at Havana
The Visual Effects Society NZ, VFX Guild of NZ and Victoria University of Wellington invite you to TEQ Talks — short, sharp talks from innovators and creatives in VFX, animation, games and beyond, sharing the topics they’re most passionate about.
After a full house in Auckland, TEQ Talks returns to Wellington — your chance to connect, share ideas, and hear from some of the sharpest minds in Aotearoa’s screen and tech scene.
Featured Speakers:
Marie Tricart & Guillaume Renier – Co-founders, 37Degrees Studio
Talk: Creativity Without Context
 Nadia Koolina & Haojun Huang – DozeFace Animation Lab
Talk: From Idea to Animation Lab: The DozeFace Story
 Raqi Syed & Areito Echevarria – Victoria University of Wellington
Talk: From Script to Screen in a Single Houdini File

Rick Stemm – PikPok
Talk: The Kungfu of Game Design

Why Attend?
Whether you’re in film, games, tech, or animation, TEQ Talks are about sparking cross-industry conversations and celebrating Aotearoa’s innovation edge.
🎟 Tickets: 
VES Members, VFXG Members, VUW Staff and Students are Free. Non-members $21 Professional / $11 Student
Spaces are limited — book now to secure your spot.
